title: Minimalist redesign with functional radar UX element, single mobile view

requirements.summary: Strip all decorative HUD elements (scanlines, gauges, grid overlays, corner brackets, vignette) and forced military jargon labels while retaining dark palette and subtle aircraft-themed language for functional elements. Ensure the entire UI fits in a single mobile viewport without scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: No scanline overlay, no gauge widgets, no grid background, no corner bracket decorations visible in the UI
No label text containing 'TACTICAL', 'MISSION BRIEFING', 'INTEL REPORT', or similar forced military jargon
The full app (header, now-playing, search, station list, favorites) fits within a single mobile viewport (375px width, 812px height) without vertical overflow
Dark background color palette is preserved
App title and functional button labels retain a subtle aviation/aircraft tone without being over-the-top

file_operations.description: Remove all decorative visual effects including the dark grid background pattern, scanline overlay pseudo-element, and radial vignette effect. Retain only a clean dark background container that wraps children without visual embellishments.

Remove forced military jargon from system status indicators and labels. Keep the 'SQUADRON RADIO' title and fighter jet logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/fighter-jet-logo.dim_256x256.png) as they serve functional branding. Simplify the header layout to be more compact for mobile, removing any decorative border effects or excessive spacing.

Remove any forced military terminology from labels and status text. Retain functional playback controls (play/pause/stop buttons, volume slider) and station metadata display. Simplify the layout to be more compact, removing decorative signal bars animation or replacing it with a simpler visual indicator. Ensure the panel uses minimal vertical space.

Strip any military-themed label text from search inputs and filter controls. Keep the functional search fields (name, genre/tag, country) with plain descriptive labels. Compact the layout to reduce vertical height, ensuring quick-select tag buttons and country dropdown remain usable but space-efficient.

Remove decorative border effects, excessive padding, and any military jargon from empty/loading state messages. Maintain functional elements (station name, play button, favorite toggle) in a clean, minimal list style. Ensure the list scrolls efficiently within the remaining viewport space after header and now-playing.
Remove the GaugeWidget component entirely as it is a purely decorative element with no functional purpose in the minimalist redesign.

Remove any references to GaugeWidget component imports and usage. Adjust the overall layout composition to ensure all active components (AppHeader, NowPlayingPanel, SearchPanel, StationList, and the repurposed RadarWidget) fit within a single mobile viewport (375px x 812px) without vertical scrolling. Apply flex layout constraints and overflow handling to enforce the single-view requirement.

Remove CSS rules for scanline overlays, corner bracket decorations, excessive glow effects, and grid background patterns. Retain only the dark color palette custom properties and minimal theme variables. Strip any forced military-styled utility classes (e.g., tactical-border, hud-glow) that are not essential for functional UI elements.

Remove or simplify custom HUD-specific animations (radar-sweep can remain for the functional radar), excessive box shadow definitions, and decorative keyframes (pulse-glow) that are not needed in the minimalist design. Keep the core dark theme colors and functional font families (Share Tech Mono, Orbitron) for subtle aircraft tone.

requirements.summary: Repurpose RadarWidget as a compact, functional UX state indicator that changes sweep speed or blip behavior based on playback state (stopped, loading, playing, error). Position it minimally (e.g., corner or inline with now-playing) without dominating layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Radar widget is visible but compact — no larger than ~80x80px in the layout
Radar sweep speed or blip behavior changes based on playback state (stopped, loading, playing, error)
Radar does not take up significant vertical space and does not break the single mobile view constraint
Removing the radar would not break any other functionality (it is purely a state indicator)

file_operations.description: Refactor the RadarWidget to accept a 'playbackState' prop (stopped, loading, playing, paused, error) and adjust the sweep animation speed or blip appearance accordingly. For example: slow sweep when stopped/idle, faster sweep or active blip when playing/loading, static or dimmed when error. Reduce the canvas size to approximately 80x80px and remove any decorative grid circles or crosshairs that do not serve the functional state indication. Keep the rotating sweep line as the primary visual feedback mechanism.

Wire the repurposed RadarWidget into the layout by passing the current playback state from useRadioPlayer hook as a prop. Position the radar compactly in a corner (e.g., top-right of the header or inline within NowPlayingPanel) so it does not dominate vertical space. Ensure the radar is visually separated but does not break the single mobile viewport constraint.
